_note: Behavioral-rules seed/template for workspace-qdrant-mcp — full recovery artifact.

Rules live in the daemon's `rules` Qdrant collection (host-bind state/qdrant/storage), NOT in git — a volume loss, `state/` wipe, or tenant change wipes them. This file is the versioned source of truth to RE-APPLY them.

Two scopes: GLOBAL rules apply to every project; PROJECT rules bind to a tenant_id (resolved from cwd). The default `rules action=list` is project-scoped — run `rules action=list scope=global` to see the global set.
Re-apply GLOBAL: for each entry in `global`, call MCP `rules` action=add with scope=global (cwd optional).
Re-apply PROJECT: for each entry in `project`, call MCP `rules` action=add with scope=project AND your repo cwd (so it binds to the right tenant_id).
Keep this file in sync whenever a rule is added/changed. Last synced: 2026-05-29.

global.label: search-first
global.title: Always search before answering
global.content: For any question about this project's code, structure, or library docs, ALWAYS call the `search` tool before answering — do not rely on training data. Defaults: scope="project", limit=10. Widen to scope="all" or includeLibraries=true only after a project-scoped query comes back empty. Use mode="semantic" for concept queries, mode="keyword" or exact=true for known identifiers.

global.label: scope-project
global.content: Start `search` calls with scope="project" and a small limit (default 10). Widen to scope="all" or includeLibraries=true only after the project-scoped query returns nothing useful. Never widen silently — the widened call is more expensive and noisier.

global.label: grep-for-exact
global.content: For exact identifiers, regex, or known substrings, use the `grep` tool before `search` — it is faster and cheaper than `search` with exact=true. Reserve `search` for semantic discovery (concepts, intent, "what does the code do around X").

global.label: branch-default
global.content: `search` operates on the current branch of the current project by default. To compare or include other branches, pass branch="<name>" or branch="*" (all branches) explicitly. Never widen branch scope silently — results from stale branches will mislead reasoning.

global.label: no-proj-write
global.content: Never call `store` targeting the `projects` collection — that collection is daemon-owned via file watching. Use `scratchpad` for ad-hoc notes, `libraries` only when the user explicitly asks, or `store` with type="project" to register/activate a project directory.

global.label: confirm-mut
global.content: Mutating `workspace_index` actions (add_project, start_agent_branch, finish_agent_branch, abandon_agent_branch, register_wqm, register_all_wqm, cleanup_orphans) require double opt-in: allowMutation:true in the call AND WQM_INDEX_MANAGER_ALLOW_MUTATION=1 in the server env. ALWAYS get explicit user confirmation before calling these — they affect persistent shared state across sessions. `sync_current_branch` is for git hooks only; agents must not call it directly.

global.label: agent-branch
global.content: When creating an agent/feature branch (especially in a parallel worktree), register it via `workspace_index` with action="start_agent_branch", passing branchName, purpose, createdBy, and useWorktree=true if applicable. Close out with finish_agent_branch (merged) or abandon_agent_branch (discarded) — never leave it dangling in the registry. This is a mutating action and requires double opt-in (see confirm-mut).

project.label: look-then-write
project.title: Inspect daemon shared state before overwriting it
project.content: Before overwriting or deleting daemon-owned shared state (global.wqmignore, named volumes, the queue/search SQLite DBs), inspect/diff the target first. The live global.wqmignore is NOT empty — it carries custom entries (e.g. `**/bws-dev-plataform/testlink/**`, `**/zabbix/zabbix/**`) that a blind truncate-overwrite would destroy. Prefer append over overwrite; diff against assets/global.wqmignore.example rather than replacing.

project.label: daemon-via-grpc
project.title: Operate the daemon via container gRPC, not host wqm
project.content: Reach the daemon through the dockerized stack — MCP gRPC, or `docker exec wqm-memexd wqm ...` for CLI ops (e.g. `wqm admin requeue-failed`). Do NOT run the host `wqm` CLI: it resolves the wrong DB (host XDG path, not the daemon's volume). Running wqm INSIDE wqm-memexd resolves the real /var/lib/memexd/memexd.db.

project.label: db-via-sidecar
project.title: Query daemon SQLite via sidecar on the active volume
project.content: Inspect/query the daemon's SQLite (memexd.db, queue/state) with a sidecar container mounting volume `workspace-qdrant-mcp_memexd_db` (RW + WAL = safe concurrent read with the live daemon). Gotchas: the compose-prefixed `workspace-qdrant-mcp_memexd_db` is live; bare `memexd_db` was a stale empty orphan (removed). The live Qdrant index is a HOST BIND at state/qdrant/storage (no qdrant volume). global.wqmignore is a host bind at state/memexd/.

project.label: validate-build
project.title: Validate the crate, then rebuild the container image
project.content: For Rust daemon changes, validate the specific crate FIRST on Windows — `cargo check/clippy/test -p workspace-qdrant-core` (or -grpc); core/grpc compile without ORT. Then deploy by rebuilding the image: `docker compose build memexd && docker compose up -d memexd` (memexd is heavy; mcp is a fast TS rebuild). The running container keeps the OLD binary until rebuilt — source edits alone do nothing.

project.label: wqmignore-edit
project.title: Edit global.wqmignore at host bind; use **/ dir prefixes
project.content: The daemon's global ignore is the host-bind file state/memexd/global.wqmignore (read at reconcile; mounted at /var/lib/memexd/global.wqmignore in BOTH the memexd and mcp containers). APPEND, never truncate — it holds custom + worktree patterns. Directory patterns MUST use the `**/` prefix: the Rust `ignore` crate's add_ignore() anchors patterns at the file's parent dir, so `.claude/` alone won't match project-level (use `**/.claude/`). Worktrees and .claude/.codex/.agents are excluded by design. Canonical template: assets/global.wqmignore.example.

project.label: vitest-scoped
project.title: Run vitest scoped to one file via cd + npx
project.content: Run a single MCP-server test with `cd src/typescript/mcp-server && npx vitest run <file-substring>`. Do NOT use `npm --prefix ... exec -- vitest --root ...` — the positional path filter is dropped and the entire ~859-test suite runs (and crashes on the server-http-transport teardown with a stack-overflow). Typecheck with `npx tsc --noEmit` from the mcp-server dir.

project.label: mcp-pass-cwd
project.title: Always pass cwd (or projectId) to workspace-qdrant tools
project.content: The MCP server runs over HTTP (dockerized) and CANNOT observe your working directory. Always pass `cwd` (absolute path) — or an explicit `projectId` — to `search`, `grep`, `list`, `retrieve`, and `rules`. Omitting both yields "Could not detect project" (the Windows/9P mount makes path-based detection fail: client sends C:\..., daemon registered /run/desktop/mnt/host/c/...).

project.label: worktree-tools
project.title: On worktrees: local Read/Grep; MCP search against main
project.content: Git worktrees (.claude/worktrees/, parallel agent worktrees) are NOT indexed by design (excluded in global.wqmignore — indexing them is ~98% noise). So inside a worktree, read its own files with the native Read/Grep tools, and use workspace-qdrant `search`/`grep` (MCP) only against the main canonical index. Merging back to main re-indexes via the file watcher.
